data.title: RH beats Q1 expectations, keeps guidance unchanged despite tariff uncertainties
data.description:
Shares of RH (NYSE:RH) are rallying in Thursday’s after-hours trading as the company’s efforts to increase investments in Europe, expand and elevate its product offerings, and mitigate tariff headwinds delivered better-than-expected results for the first quarter.

For the quarter ended May 3, the luxury home furnishings' retailer earned an adjusted profit of $0.13 per share. This compares to a loss of $0.40 in the same quarter last year and beat expectations for a loss of $0.07. And despite operating in the “worst housing market in 50 years,” sales were up 12% to an above-consensus $814M.

Adjusted operating margin improved by 50 basis points to 7% while adjusted EBITDA margin increased 80 basis points to 13.1%, both of which were at the high end of the company’s expectations.

Looking ahead to the current quarter and fiscal year 2025, RH (NYSE:RH) continues to take steps to mitigate the impact of U.S. import tariff with a goal to shift sourcing from China to just 2% from 16%, currently, with a “meaningful portion” of costs to be absorbed by vendors.

The company will also delay the launch of the new concept planned for the second half of 2025 to the spring of 2026 when there is more certainty regarding tariffs.

Additionally, due to the “significant and unexpected” Liberation Day tariffs announced on April 2, the disruption in shipments and resourcing efforts will negatively impact revenues by ~6 points in Q2 and will be recovered in the second half of the year.  

For the second quarter, revenue is expected to increase by 8% to 10%, translating into Q2 revenue of $896.03M to $912.63M, below the $915.8M estimate. Adjusted operating margin is pegged for 15% to 16% and adjusted operating margin 15% to 16%.

The company expects FY25 revenue growth of 10% to 13%, translating to $3.5B to $3.6B versus $3.52B estimates. Adjusted operating margin is expected to be between 14% to 15% and adjusted EBITDA margin of 20% to 21%. Free cash flow is targeted to be between $250M to $350M.

“While there remains uncertainty until the reciprocal tariff negotiations are complete, we have proven we are well positioned to compete favorably in any market conditions,” the company said in a statement.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Nano Dimension's Q1 loss decreases 33%
Forum Energy Technologies to supply submarine rescue system for Indonesia Navy
data.description:
Forum Energy Technologies (NYSE:FET) said Thursday it was awarded a contract to supply a state-of-the-art submarine rescue vehicle system for use by Indonesia's Navy; financial details were not disclosed.

The new LR600 model SRV, a piloted rescue submersible, will be capable of operating at depths of up to 600 meters, carrying 20 people, and will be fully integrated with a hyperbaric rescue facility while also utilizing the latest technologies to locate a distressed submarine and quickly transfer personnel, the company said, adding that sea trials are planned for 2028.

The SRV will be built at Forum Energy's (FET) facility in Kirkbymoorside, Yorkshire, England, and the bespoke LARS will be manufactured at the company's Bryan, Texas, facility.

data.title: Chime Financial stock jumps 37% in first day of trading
data.description:
Chime Financial (NASDAQ:CHYM) stock closed at $37.11 on its first day of trading, 37% above its IPO price of $27.

At Thursday's closing price, Chime Financial (NASDAQ:CHYM) gets a market capitalization of ~$13.5B, as it has about 364.4M shares outstanding after the IPO.

Chime (CHYM) works with national banks to offer banking products and services. The banking app doesn't rely on fees such as overdraft, monthly service, or minimum balance fees, it said on its website. Rather, it generates revenue when members spend using a Chime-branded debit or credit card, based on fees paid via the card networks, rather than fees paid to us by its members.

data.title: Equifax secures 35 new patents in first half of 2025 in technology-development push
data.description:
Equifax (NYSE:EFX) has added 35 new patents in the first half of the year, it said on Thursday, bringing the company's total number of issued or pending patents to nearly 650 patents spanning 15 countries.

Its patents, overall, encompass distinctive techniques in artificial intelligence and machine learning, data & analytics, cybersecurity, and identity & fraud solutions.

The latest technology and innovation covered by the most recent Equifax (NYSE:EFX) patents include: automated model development process; automatically generating search index for expediting searching of a computerized database; detecting synthetic online entities facilitated by primary entities; production-ready attributes creation and management for software development; techniques for determining legitimacy of email addresses for online access control; and updating attribute data structures to indicate trends in attribute data provided to automated modeling systems.

"With innovation made possible by the Equifax Cloud, our latest patents reflect our continued commitment to developing new technologies and solutions that empower our customers to drive greater access to financial opportunity to more people in more places," said Harald Schneider, Chief Data & Analytics Officer at Equifax.

data.title: Granite selected for Tahoe Cedars water reconstruction project
data.description:
• Granite (NYSE:GVA) said on Thursday that it has been selected by the Tahoe City Public Utility District for the approximately $3 million preconstruction portion of the Tahoe Cedars Water Reconstruction Project. 
  • Preconstruction activities commenced this Spring and are expected to run through early 2026, and the anticipated construction value of the contract is $60 million.
  • The construction portion of the project is expected to begin in May 2026 and continue through 2029.
  • Granite (NYSE:GVA) shares were up 0.57% in extended trading.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Adobe raises FY25 targets after strong Q2 print
Adobe's Q2 results surpass estimates as it increases guidance on AI traction
data.description:
Adobe (NASDAQ:ADBE) released its second quarter fiscal 2025 financial results post-market on Thursday, which easily surpassed expectations.

The company also increased its guidance due to widespread adoption of its artificial intelligence offerings.

For the quarter ended May 30, the software giant reported adjusted earnings per share of $5.06, which was more than the consensus estimate of $4.97. It reported GAAP EPS of $3.94, which surpassed the estimate of $3.87.

It reported revenue of $5.87B versus the estimate of $5.80B. This represented a year-over-year increase of 11% and marked a record high in quarterly revenue.

Looking ahead, Adobe expects third quarter adjusted EPS ranging from $5.15 to $5.20, which is well above the estimate of $5.11. It projects revenue to range from $5.875B to $5.925B, with a mid-point of $5.9B eclipsing the $5.88B consensus.

And for the entirety of fiscal 2025, Adobe forecasts revenue ranging from $23.5B to $23.6B, which is more than the estimate of $23.45B. It expects adjusted earnings per share to range from $20.50 to $20.70, which is well above the $20.39 estimate.

"As a result of us driving strong performance in the first half of the year, we are pleased to raise Adobe’s FY25 total revenue and EPS targets," said Dan Durn, Adobe's executive vice president and CFO. "We continue to invest in AI innovation across our customer groups to enhance value realization and expand the universe of customers we serve."

Adobe exited the quarter with remaining performance obligations totalling $19.69B. The company also revealed it had repurchased 8.6M shares during the second quarter.

"Adobe's AI innovation is transforming industries, enabling individuals and enterprises to achieve unprecedented levels of creativity," said CEO Shantanu Narayen.

Adobe's Digital Media segment increased 11% year over year to $4.35B, while its Digital Experience segment climbed 10% year over year to $1.46B.
